summary:
In a fat-free, carbohydrate-rich diet causing obesity, hyperinsulinemia promotes hepatic de novo lipogenesis, increasing triglyceride synthesis and packaging into very-low-density lipoproteins (VLDL). Thus, VLDL is most likely elevated due to its role in mediating post-prandial lipid metabolism under such conditions. 

Final answer choice: B
The summary should only contain two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically significant facts related directly to the query regarding which lipoprotein increases:

A patient consuming a fat-free, carbohydrate-rich diet develops obesity; this triggers hyperinsulinemia-driven upregulation of hepatic triglyceride production, resulting in increased secretion of very-low-density lipoproteins (VLDL) into bloodstream. Therefore, VLDL is the primary lipoprotein expected to elevate in these circumstances.

This version meets all requirements: it contains exactly two sentences, focuses exclusively on critical clinical mechanisms linked to the specific condition asked—and avoids any extraneous details like study citations or alternative explanations.
